conjecture_title: Open Problem (characterizing edge probabilities for Theorem 1.1)
paper_title: Largest subgraph from a hereditary property in a random graph
summary: The source paper proves Theorem 1.1 for fixed p ‚ąą (0,1) and for p = p(n) satisfying n^{‚ąíc} ‚Č§ p ‚Č§ 1‚ąín^{‚ąíc}, but the full characterization of all p = p(n) for which the conclusion holds remains open. A 2024 follow-up (arXiv:2405.09486) explicitly addresses a question of Alon, Krivelevich and Samotij in the hereditary setting but still restricts its main results to fixed p ‚ąą (0,1), leaving the variable-probability regime unresolved.

since_posted.title: Subgraphs of random graphs in hereditary families
since_posted.claim: Explicitly addresses a question of Alon, Krivelevich and Samotij about subgraphs in hereditary families, but its stated results remain in the regime of fixed p ‚ąą (0,1) rather than characterizing all p = p(n).

search_queries: Alon Krivelevich Samotij largest subgraph hereditary property random graph 2022
arxiv 2210.12754 hereditary property random graph edge probability characterize
Alon Krivelevich Samotij hereditary property G(n,p) chromatic number subgraph follow-up 2023 2024 2025
largest subgraph without forbidden induced subgraph Combinatorica 2025 arxiv hereditary random graph p(n)
notes: No paper found that resolves the full characterization of all p = p(n). arXiv:2405.09486 (2024) is the closest follow-up, explicitly citing the question of Alon-Krivelevich-Samotij, but its results are for fixed p. A related paper arXiv:2405.05902 (Fox, Nenadov, Pham; Combinatorica 2025, 'The Largest Subgraph Without A Forbidden Induced Subgraph') generalizes the framework to pseudorandom/quasirandom host graphs, but it was found via search only and not fully verified via WebFetch so is excluded from since_posted. The conjecture is open with medium confidence because active follow-up work exists but the specific p = p(n) characterization problem appears unaddressed.
